𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗩𝗢𝗟𝗨𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗔𝗟-𝗛𝗔𝗡𝗗𝗪𝗥𝗜𝗧𝗜𝗡𝗚-𝗚𝗔𝗡 | Generating realistic, variable-length handwritten text using semi-supervised learning techniques.  📈 Growing!

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Generating realistic, variable-length handwritten text using semi-supervised learning techniques.
📅 Created: May 2020 • Updated: Jun 2026

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

✅ Semi-supervised handwritten text generation
✅ Varying length text generation
✅ PyTorch implementation
✅ Supervised and semi-supervised training modes
✅ Data preparation scripts for common datasets (IAM, RIMES, CVL)
✅ Visdom integration for visualization during training

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

ScrabbleGAN is a PyTorch implementation for semi-supervised handwritten text generation. It focuses on generating realistic varying-length handwritten text, leveraging both labeled and unlabeled data for improved performance. The project includes scripts for supervised and semi-supervised training, dependency management, and data preparation.

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a native desktop application for controlling and monitoring Home Assistant devices on Windows and Linux, offering a more integrated and accessible experience than a web browser.

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Prism Desktop is a lightweight, customizable Home Assistant client for Windows and Linux, offering system tray integration and real-time control of smart home devices without needing a web browser. It leverages Home Assistant's WebSocket API to provide instant updates and allows users to manage lights, thermostats, cameras, and automations through a sleek, interactive dashboard.

Netviz is a browser-based application for visually designing network architectures. It allows users to add, connect, and customize network components like servers and databases, offering features for organization, custom icons, and local saving without a server.

𝗩𝗢𝗜𝗖𝗘𝗕𝗢𝗫 | To provide users with a comprehensive, privacy-focused, and locally-run AI voice studio for voice cloning, speech generation, and voice input.  🌟 Rising Fast!

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide users with a comprehensive, privacy-focused, and locally-run AI voice studio for voice cloning, speech generation, and voice input.
📅 Created: Jan 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿✿

✅ Local-first AI voice studio
✅ Voice cloning (zero-shot)
✅ Speech generation (7 TTS engines, 23 languages)
✅ Voice input (global dictation hotkey)
✅ Post-processing effects
✅ Expressive speech control
✅ Unlimited audio length with auto-chunking
✅ Stories editor (multi-track timeline)
✅ Complete privacy (runs locally)

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Voicebox is an open-source, local-first AI voice studio that allows users to clone voices, generate speech in multiple languages and TTS engines, and use voice input for dictation. It provides a complete voice I/O stack running locally for enhanced privacy and control, offering an alternative to cloud-based services like ElevenLabs and WisprFlow.

𝗦𝗞𝗜𝗟𝗟𝗦 | To enhance the functionality and reliability of AI coding agents by providing a set of practical, adaptable skills that improve alignment, reduce verbosity, and facilitate better communication between developers and agents.  🔥 Super Hot!

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 Shell 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To enhance the functionality and reliability of AI coding agents by providing a set of practical, adaptable skills that improve alignment, reduce verbosity, and facilitate better communication between developers and agents.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖❖

✅ Shell-based agent skills for coding agents
✅ Composable and adaptable skills
✅ Addresses common agent failure modes (misalignment, verbosity)
✅ Includes skills like `/grill-me` and `/grill-with-docs` for detailed alignment
✅ Facilitates creation of a shared language document for agents
✅ Quickstart installer via `npx skills@latest add mattpocock/skills`
✅ Configurable issue tracker integration (GitHub, Linear, local files)

This repository provides a collection of shell-based 'skills' for real engineers, designed to enhance the capabilities of coding agents. These skills are intended to be small, adaptable, and composable, aiming to improve agent performance by addressing common failure modes like misalignment and verbosity.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automating the development process by having an AI agent iteratively complete tasks defined in a Product Requirements Document.
📅 Created: Jan 2026 • Updated: Jun 2026

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Autonomous AI agent loop for task completion
✅ Supports Amp and Claude Code AI tools
✅ Context persistence via git history, progress.txt, and prd.json
✅ Iterative execution with fresh AI instances per loop
✅ Workflow for PRD generation, conversion to JSON, and execution
✅ Automatic branch creation, story implementation, and quality checks
✅ Learning accumulation in progress.txt


◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 19.9K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 33 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Ralph is an autonomous AI agent loop designed to repeatedly execute coding tasks until all Product Requirements Document (PRD) items are completed. It leverages AI coding tools like Amp or Claude Code, maintaining context through git history, progress files, and a structured PRD JSON format.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈
